diagrams.title: Binary Tree Terminology
diagrams.description: A labeled binary tree illustrating all key terms: root, parent, child, leaf, sibling, ancestor, descendant, depth, and height.
elements: Root node (A): topmost node with no parent
Leaf nodes (D, E, F): nodes with no children
Parent-Child: A is parent of B and C; B and C are children of A
Siblings: B and C are siblings (same parent A)
Depth: A=0, B=1, D=2; Height: D=0, B=1, A=2
Internal nodes: A, B, C have at least one child
Degree: A=2, B=2, C=2, D=0, E=0, F=0
diagrams.purpose: Clear visualization of tree terminology helps in understanding tree properties and traversals. Essential for exam questions on tree definitions.

diagrams.title: BST Insertion Process
diagrams.description: Step-by-step BST construction inserting: 50, 30, 70, 20, 40, 60, 80 showing how ordering property maintains search efficiency.
elements: Insert 50 as root
Insert 30 < 50 → left of 50
Insert 70 > 50 → right of 50
Insert 20 < 50, < 30 → left of 30
Insert 40 < 50, > 30 → right of 30
Insert 60 > 50, < 70 → left of 70
Insert 80 > 50, > 70 → right of 70
Final tree structure with proper BST property
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates BST insertion algorithm and how the ordering property (left < parent < right) is maintained at each step. Shows resulting tree shape.

diagrams.title: Tree Traversals Visualized
diagrams.description: Single tree with traversal paths highlighted for Preorder, Inorder, and Postorder showing order of node visits.
elements: Tree: Root 10, left 5, right 15, 5 has left 2, right 7; 15 has left 12, right 20
Numbered path diagram for each traversal type
diagrams.purpose: Helps students visualize the difference between traversals and remember their orders. Inorder on BST gives sorted sequence - important concept.
